title: malloc() — allocate bytes32 buffer without zeroing memory, free() conditionally restores pointer
trigger_patterns: allocate fixed size buffer in assembly
dynamic array allocation without zero init
stack too deep workaround with buffer
solidity_before:
// Solidity always zeros new memory
bytes32[] memory buffer = new bytes32[](10); // zeros 10 slots
explanation: malloc skips Solidity's memory zeroing — caller is responsible for writing every slot before reading. free() uses a branchless trick: the mstore destination is either 0x40 (restoring the free pointer to buffer start) or 0x00 (a no-op write to scratch space). The condition shl(6, ...) produces either 64 (0x40) or 0 depending on whether the buffer is still at the top of memory.
gas_saved_unit: per allocation (saves 32 gas per slot not zeroed)
when_to_apply: Temporary buffers where all slots will be written before being read, stack-too-deep workarounds
when_not_to_apply: Buffers returned to callers or passed to untrusted code — uninitialized memory may leak previous values
